fields.name: Mombasa
fields.description: Mombasa County is Kenya's smallest county by area (212.5 km˛) and a major port city on the Indian Ocean. Capital Mombasa. Population ~1.2 million (2019). Key economic drivers: trade, tourism, shipping, and manufacturing. The county serves as a regional commercial hub with the Port of Mombasa.

fields.name: Kwale
fields.description: Kwale County lies along the coast south of Mombasa, covering 8,270 km˛. Capital Kwale. Population ~867,000 (2019). Gateway county with proximity to Mombasa port and the Great Northern Corridor. Economy: tourism (Diani Beach), agriculture, mining (titanium), and trade.

fields.name: Kilifi
fields.description: Kilifi County stretches along the coast north of Mombasa, 12,246 km˛. Capital Kilifi. Population ~1.45 million (2019). Known for Malindi and Watamu beach tourism, fishing, and coconut farming. Home to Kilifi Creek and diverse marine ecosystems.

fields.name: Tana River
fields.description: Tana River County is Kenya's fourth-largest by area (35,376 km˛), traversed by the Tana River. Capital Hola. Population ~316,000 (2019). Semi-arid; economy based on pastoralism, irrigated agriculture along the Tana, and wildlife (Tana River Primate Reserve).
fields.governor_name: H.E Maj. (Rtd) Dr. Dhadho Gaddae Godhana

fields.name: Lamu
fields.description: Lamu County includes the Lamu Archipelago on the northern coast. Area 6,498 km˛, population ~144,000 (2019). Capital Lamu Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Economy: tourism, fishing, and dhow building. Known for Swahili culture and historic architecture.

fields.name: Taita-Taveta
fields.description: Taita-Taveta County borders Tanzania, 17,084 km˛. Capital Mwatate. Population ~341,000 (2019). Home to Tsavo East and West National Parks. Economy: tourism, mining (gemstones), and agriculture. Taita Hills are a biodiversity hotspot.

fields.name: Garissa
fields.description: Garissa County is in North Eastern Kenya, 45,720 km˛. Capital Garissa. Population ~841,000 (2019). Semi-arid; economy based on pastoralism and livestock. Key priorities: infrastructure, trade, and improving agricultural productivity.

fields.name: Wajir
fields.description: Wajir County is Kenya's largest by area (55,841 km˛). Capital Wajir. Population ~781,000 (2019). Arid; pastoral economy (camels, goats, sheep). Part of the former North Eastern Province.

fields.name: Mandera
fields.description: Mandera County borders Ethiopia and Somalia, 25,798 km˛. Capital Mandera. Population ~867,000 (2019). Arid; pastoral economy. Strategic border location for cross-border trade.

fields.name: Marsabit
fields.description: Marsabit County is Kenya's largest county by area (66,923 km˛). Capital Marsabit. Population ~460,000 (2019). Includes Marsabit National Park and Lake Turkana. Economy: pastoralism, tourism, and small-scale agriculture.

fields.name: Isiolo
fields.description: Isiolo County lies at the crossroads of northern Kenya, 25,336 km˛. Capital Isiolo. Population ~268,000 (2019). Gateway to northern parks; economy: pastoralism, tourism (Samburu, Buffalo Springs), and trade.

fields.name: Meru
fields.description: Meru County is in the eastern highlands, 7,003 km˛. Capital Meru Town. Population ~1.55 million (2019). Fertile highlands; economy: coffee, tea, miraa, dairy, and horticulture. Home to Mount Kenya's eastern slopes.

fields.name: Tharaka-Nithi
fields.description: Tharaka-Nithi County was carved from Meru, 2,610 km˛. Capital Kathwana. Population ~393,000 (2019). Economy: agriculture (miraa, coffee, livestock) and small-scale trade.

fields.name: Embu
fields.description: Embu County lies on the eastern slopes of Mount Kenya, 2,556 km˛. Capital Embu. Population ~609,000 (2019). Economy: coffee, tea, dairy, and horticulture. Fertile volcanic soils.

fields.name: Kitui
fields.description: Kitui County is in eastern Kenya, 24,385 km˛. Capital Kitui. Population ~1.14 million (2019). Semi-arid; economy: livestock, drought-resistant crops, and mining (coal exploration).

fields.name: Machakos
fields.description: Machakos County is near Nairobi, 5,953 km˛. Capital Machakos. Population ~1.42 million (2019). Growing satellite town; economy: agriculture, trade, and manufacturing. Part of the greater Nairobi metropolitan area.

fields.name: Makueni
fields.description: Makueni County is in southeastern Kenya, 8,009 km˛. Capital Wote. Population ~988,000 (2019). Semi-arid; economy: livestock, drought-resistant crops, and bee-keeping. Tsavo East borders the county.

fields.name: Nyandarua
fields.description: Nyandarua County is in the Central Highlands, 3,108 km˛. Capital Ol Kalou. Population ~638,000 (2019). Aberdare Range; economy: dairy, potatoes, pyrethrum, and tourism.
fields.governor_name: H.E Dr. Moses N. Badilisha Kiarie

fields.name: Nyeri
fields.description: Nyeri County lies on the southern slopes of Mount Kenya, 2,361 km˛. Capital Nyeri. Population ~759,000 (2019). Fertile; economy: coffee, tea, dairy, and horticulture. Home to Aberdare National Park.

fields.name: Kirinyaga
fields.description: Kirinyaga County is on Mount Kenya's southeastern slopes, 1,205 km˛. Capital Kerugoya. Population ~610,000 (2019). Economy: rice (Mwea), coffee, tea, and horticulture. Mwea Irrigation Scheme is a major rice producer.

fields.name: Murang'a
fields.description: Murang'a County is in the Central Highlands, 2,326 km˛. Capital Murang'a. Population ~1.06 million (2019). Economy: coffee, tea, dairy, and horticulture. Birthplace of Kenya's independence movement.

fields.name: Kiambu
fields.description: Kiambu County borders Nairobi to the north, 2,449 km˛. Capital Kiambu. Population ~2.42 million (2019). Part of Nairobi metro; economy: horticulture, dairy, coffee, and real estate.

fields.name: Nairobi
fields.description: Nairobi is Kenya's capital and largest city, 695 km˛. Population ~4.4 million (2019). Economic powerhouse contributing ~27.5% of Kenya's GDP. Home to Parliament, UNEP, and Nairobi National Park—the only capital with a national park.

fields.name: Turkana
fields.description: Turkana County is Kenya's largest by area (98,598 km˛). Capital Lodwar. Population ~1.5 million (2019). Lake Turkana, oil exploration. Economy: pastoralism, fishing, and emerging oil sector.

fields.name: West Pokot
fields.description: West Pokot County borders Uganda, 8,418 km˛. Capital Kapenguria. Population ~621,000 (2019). Economy: livestock, agriculture, and mining. Known for the Pokot people and Cherangani Hills.

fields.name: Samburu
fields.description: Samburu County is in the northern Rift Valley, 20,183 km˛. Capital Maralal. Population ~310,000 (2019). Economy: pastoralism and tourism (Samburu National Reserve). Home to the Samburu people.

fields.name: Trans Nzoia
fields.description: Trans Nzoia County is in the Rift Valley, 2,470 km˛. Capital Kitale. Population ~990,000 (2019). 'Breadbasket of Kenya'; economy: maize, wheat, dairy, and horticulture.

fields.name: Uasin Gishu
fields.description: Uasin Gishu County is in the Rift Valley, 2,955 km˛. Capital Eldoret. Population ~1.16 million (2019). Eldoret is a major hub; economy: agriculture, athletics (training camps), and trade.

fields.name: Elgeyo-Marakwet
fields.description: Elgeyo-Marakwet County is in the Rift Valley, 3,050 km˛. Capital Iten. Population ~454,000 (2019). 'Home of Champions'—world-famous for long-distance runners. Economy: athletics, agriculture, and tourism.

fields.name: Nandi
fields.description: Nandi County is in the Rift Valley, 2,885 km˛. Capital Kapsabet. Population ~886,000 (2019). Birthplace of many Olympic champions. Economy: tea, maize, dairy, and athletics tourism.

fields.name: Baringo
fields.description: Baringo County lies in the Rift Valley, 11,075 km˛. Capital Kabarnet. Population ~667,000 (2019). Lake Baringo and Lake Bogoria; economy: tourism, fishing, and pastoralism.

fields.name: Laikipia
fields.description: Laikipia County is in the central Rift Valley, 8,696 km˛. Capital Rumuruti. Population ~519,000 (2019). Wildlife conservancies; economy: tourism, ranching, and conservation.

fields.name: Nakuru
fields.description: Nakuru County is in the Rift Valley, 7,510 km˛. Capital Nakuru. Population ~2.16 million (2019). Lake Nakuru (flamingos), Menengai Crater. Economy: agriculture, tourism, and manufacturing.

fields.name: Narok
fields.description: Narok County is in the Rift Valley, 17,921 km˛. Capital Narok. Population ~1.16 million (2019). Maasai Mara National Reserve; economy: tourism, livestock, and wheat farming.

fields.name: Kajiado
fields.description: Kajiado County borders Tanzania, 21,293 km˛. Capital Kajiado. Population ~1.12 million (2019). Part of Nairobi metro; economy: livestock, mining (soda ash), and tourism (Amboseli).

fields.name: Kericho
fields.description: Kericho County is in the South Rift, 2,455 km˛. Capital Kericho. Population ~902,000 (2019). Kenya's tea capital; major plantations. Economy: tea, forestry, and dairy.

fields.name: Bomet
fields.description: Bomet County is in the South Rift, 1,998 km˛. Capital Bomet. Population ~876,000 (2019). Economy: tea, dairy, maize, and horticulture. Part of the tea-growing highlands.

fields.name: Kakamega
fields.description: Kakamega County is in Western Kenya, 3,034 km˛. Capital Kakamega. Population ~1.87 million (2019). Kakamega Forest—Kenya's only tropical rainforest. Economy: agriculture, forestry, and trade.

fields.name: Vihiga
fields.description: Vihiga County is Kenya's smallest by area (531 km˛). Capital Mbale. Population ~590,000 (2019). Densely populated; economy: tea, sugarcane, and subsistence farming.

fields.name: Bungoma
fields.description: Bungoma County is in Western Kenya, 2,207 km˛. Capital Bungoma. Population ~1.67 million (2019). Economy: sugarcane, maize, and dairy. Nzoia Sugar Company is a major employer.

fields.name: Busia
fields.description: Busia County borders Uganda, 1,628 km˛. Capital Busia. Population ~894,000 (2019). Border town; economy: cross-border trade, agriculture, and fishing (Lake Victoria).

fields.name: Siaya
fields.description: Siaya County is in Nyanza, 2,496 km˛. Capital Siaya. Population ~993,000 (2019). Birthplace of Oginga Odinga. Economy: fishing, rice, sugarcane, and trade.

fields.name: Kisumu
fields.description: Kisumu County is on Lake Victoria, 2,010 km˛. Capital Kisumu. Population ~1.16 million (2019). Kenya's third-largest city; economy: fishing, trade, sugarcane, and services. Luo cultural center.

fields.name: Homa Bay
fields.description: Homa Bay County is on Lake Victoria, 3,155 km˛. Capital Homa Bay. Population ~1.13 million (2019). Economy: fishing, rice farming, and sugarcane. Ruma National Park.
fields.women_rep_name: Hon. Dr. Joyce Atieno Bensuda Osogo

fields.name: Migori
fields.description: Migori County borders Tanzania, 2,586 km˛. Capital Migori. Population ~1.12 million (2019). Economy: gold mining, sugarcane, fishing, and trade. Macalder gold mines.

fields.name: Kisii
fields.description: Kisii County is in the highlands of Nyanza, 1,318 km˛. Capital Kisii. Population ~1.27 million (2019). Densely populated; economy: tea, bananas, soapstone carving, and trade.

fields.name: Nyamira
fields.description: Nyamira County was carved from Kisii, 913 km˛. Capital Nyamira. Population ~606,000 (2019). Economy: tea, pyrethrum, and dairy. Part of the Gusii highlands.

fields.name: Nominated (Nationwide)
fields.description: Nominated / special-interest senators

fields.name: United Democratic Alliance
fields.history:
The United Democratic Alliance (UDA) was officially rebranded and adopted its current name in December 2020. It was previously known as the Party of Development and Reforms (PDR), which was originally established as the Party of Action (POA) in February 2012. It is associated with Kenya's President William Ruto. Original Formation: Originally registered as Party of Action in 2012.
Name Change: Renamed to UDA in December 2020. The party served as the main vehicle for William Ruto in the 2022 Kenyan general election.

fields.quote: The security in Vihiga county is so poor. People are bieng shot live in Luanda, Emuhaya and Vihiga. The PS and CS must up their game in Vihiga

Contributed to debate on the Coffee Bill (Senate Bills No.10 of 2023), supporting:

Strengthened research funding

Improved sector competitiveness

Investment in agricultural innovation
